news.headline: WGS Stock Slumps After Hours As Firm Predicts Lower Test Volume For Year
news.summary: GeneDx shares fell after hours after the company signaled softer full-year exome and genome test volume than previously expected. The warning deepened demand concerns first flagged with the Q1 miss and is the freshest negative operational catalyst heading into the Q2 print.

news.headline: GeneDx (NASDAQ:WGS) Downgraded by Zacks Research to "Strong Sell"
news.summary: Zacks Research cut its rating on GeneDx from Hold to Strong Sell in early July, adding to a wave of price-target cuts (in some cases of $25-90) as analysts reset revenue growth and margin assumptions after the Q1 guidance reset. The downgrade underscores continued sell-side caution even as a few firms like Guggenheim and Piper Sandler stayed constructive.

news.headline: GeneDx price target raised to $63 from $50 at Piper Sandler on coverage policy
news.summary: Piper Sandler lifted its price target to $63 from $50 after Carelon codified outpatient genome coverage, calling it a structural ASP de-risking event. The firm estimates the policy could add roughly $100 of incremental average selling price by the second half of 2027 as the exome-to-genome mix shifts.

news.headline: GeneDx Names Mark Gardner President to Lead Its Next Phase of Scale and Growth
news.summary: GeneDx appointed Mark Gardner as President, effective June 15, 2026, reporting to CEO Katherine Stueland to lead commercial and operations at scale. The hire, a diagnostics veteran of Quest Diagnostics and Thermo Fisher, came amid the guidance reset and litigation, positioning new management to rebuild execution credibility.

news.headline: Carelon adds outpatient genome sequencing coverage, lifting insured coverage to ~80%
news.summary: Carelon, the largest U.S. lab benefit manager, added genome sequencing under the same clinical criteria as exome for undiagnosed rare disease, raising insured genome coverage to nearly 80%. It is a structural volume and reimbursement tailwind and arguably the most important positive catalyst of the period, underpinning the view that the sell-off went too far.

news.headline: Hedge Funder Eli Casdin Is Loading Up on GeneDx Shares
news.summary: Casdin Capital bought roughly 690,000 GeneDx shares for about $32.6 million over recent weeks, becoming the largest institutional holder with 3.7 million shares. The board-linked specialist's open-market accumulation into the crash is read as a contrarian vote of confidence against the litigation-driven sell-off.

catalysts.label: Warrant expiration (WGSWW)
catalysts.detail: Expiration of GeneDx warrants on July 22, 2026, a technical factor for capital structure and potential dilution.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings (est.)
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly report; the market is watching test volume, revised full-year guidance ($475-490M), and Fabric Genomics cleanup after the July preannouncement of softer volume.

catalysts.label: ASHG 2026 Annual Meeting research showcase
catalysts.detail: GeneDx will present 14 research studies (Oct 20-24, Montreal) drawn from its GeneDx Infinity dataset of nearly one million exomes/genomes, covering newborn screening, neurodevelopmental disorders, and diagnostic technology advances.

catalysts.label: Reimbursement coverage expansion / ASP uplift
catalysts.detail: Following Carelon's genome coverage policy, GeneDx has added Medicaid coverage in additional states (38 states now cover outpatient exome/genome sequencing); Piper Sandler sees ~$100 incremental ASP by H2 2027 as the exome-to-genome mix shifts.

pulse: GeneDx remains in a high-risk, headline-driven phase after the Q1 2026 miss and cut to $475-490M full-year guidance tied to a roughly 94% write-off on the Fabric Genomics acquisition, which drove a near-49% stock crash from its early-June record. A July 10 warning of softer full-year test volume and a July 1 Zacks downgrade to Strong Sell kept sentiment negative, even as structural positives accumulate: Carelon's move to cover outpatient genome sequencing (lifting insured coverage to roughly 80%) plus expanded Medicaid coverage, a Piper Sandler target hike to $63, and specialist Eli Casdin's aggressive open-market buying. New President Mark Gardner, on board since mid-June, must now stabilize execution ahead of the July 22 warrant expiration, the still-unconfirmed early-August Q2 print, and GeneDx's October ASHG research showcase. As of July 14, no new operating or regulatory news has emerged since the July 10 volume warning; the only subsequent activity has been continued law-firm solicitations tied to the pending securities litigation's August 3 lead-plaintiff deadline, which are excluded here as non-substantive.
